data.content:
Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` have incremental build problems:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild has no way to skip it, so it runs every build.
- **Volatile content** — `DateTime.Now` produces a different value every time, changing `build-timestamp.txt` and invalidating downstream targets (like `CoreCompile`), forcing a full recompile even when no source changed.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — always re-runs.
- **Always overwrites `git-hash.g.cs`** — even if the git hash hasn't changed, `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` updates the file timestamp, which triggers `CoreCompile` again.
- **Neither target registers files in `FileWrites`** — so `dotnet clean` won't remove the generated files.

### Fixes

Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` to both targets, eliminate the volatile timestamp (or make it deterministic), and use `WriteOnlyWhenDifferent` for the git hash file so content-identical rewrites don't bump the timestamp:

```xml
<!-- GenerateTimestamp: if you truly need it, gate it on project file changes -->
<Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFullPath)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt">
  ...
</Target>

<!-- EmbedGitHash: only rewrite when hash actually changes -->
<Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFullPath)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
  ...
  <WriteLinesToFile ... WriteOnlyWhenDifferent="true" />
  <ItemGroup>
    <FileWrites Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Target>
```

The `GenerateTimestamp` target is the worst offender — the volatile `DateTime.Now` defeats incremental builds entirely since the output always differs.
data.reasoningText:
I can see two custom targets with incremental build issues. Let me analyze them:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** (line 6-11): This target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild will run it every time. Worse, it writes a file with `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` which produces a different value every build, meaning the file always changes, which would invalidate downstream targets like `CoreCompile`.

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** (line 13-23): This target also has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so it runs every time. 

The `EmbedGitHash` target executes a git command to fetch the current commit hash and writes it to a generated C# file. Even if the hash hasn't changed, the target still runs and overwrites the file, causing the timestamp to update and potentially triggering unnecessary recompilation.

The core problems are that neither target declares `Inputs`/`Outputs` for incremental build support, `GenerateTimestamp` uses a volatile property that changes every build regardless of actual changes, and `EmbedGitHash` always reruns the git command and rewrites the file even when the hash is unchanged.

Additionally, neither target registers its output files in `FileWrites` for proper clean build support.
